items.detail: Server starts and registers all ten tools
items.summary: The ten-tool server registration passed as part of the 124-test suite; prepare_memory_diff is registered as idempotent, non-destructive source capture.

items.detail: Contract vocabularies equal the live database DDL
items.summary: Contract schema and live DDL vocabularies passed.

items.detail: Duplication results use only the internal graph
items.summary: Duplication output contained only internal graph results and no external field.

items.detail: Semantic match returns matched / possible / no-match correctly
items.summary: UI-aligned local concept matching returned match for a paraphrase and open for an unrelated question.

items.detail: Abandoned findings surface as a first-class result with their reason
items.summary: F-093 surfaced as abandoned with its reason and UI boundary spelling.

items.detail: No tool output contains the word 'novel'
items.summary: Executable output scan passed for duplication and wiki output.

items.detail: write_finding is subject to the same validation gate as the reviewed-write path
items.summary: MCP writes reuse write_payload and reject missing abandoned reasons and unknown sessions.

items.detail: Cross-indication recall — a LUSC question finds the LUAD finding
items.summary: A LUSC question recalled the LUAD signature finding.

items.detail: No external literature client, cache, or result field exists
items.summary: The MCP exposes internal sources only and creates no literature cache table.

items.detail: render_wiki output is reproducible from the graph and cites its findings
items.summary: Repeated wiki renders were identical and cited graph finding IDs.

items.detail: Biologist confirms a recalled match is a genuine scientific match
Surprise metrics are deterministic for fixed belief and action samples
items.summary: 24 tests passed for Beta fit, KL and entropy in bits, belief shift, equal sample counts, label normalization, and action divergence.

items.detail: Knowledge writes enforce a separate approval actor, logged approved-model elicitation, source evidence, retry identity, linear patches, and abandoned reasons
items.summary: 10 gate tests passed for separate approval, elicitation provenance, source evidence, balanced sampling, retry conflicts, linear patches, and server-only metrics.

items.detail: Knowledge recall fuses local BM25 and model-versioned dense rankings, then applies field coverage, typed condition applicability, aliases, strict-scope opt-in, and patch history
items.summary: 4 recall tests passed. With zero BM25/token overlap, pinned local model BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 retrieved the synthetic buffer_pH < 7.0 decision at cosine 0.616973. Fresh ephemeral Codex thread 019f56ed-8b03-7210-9a1d-a937de0b3c01 called the MCP tool and recommended Protocol B; aliases, applicability, strict scope, and patch history remain deterministic.

items.detail: Expert lookup returns concise source-linked evidence, separates investigation activity, and excludes off-disease, low-coverage, and non-person matches
items.summary: 6 deterministic tests passed. Fresh ephemeral Codex thread 019f5754-1efb-7893-a059-694bc943813b returned only Roopal for scoped BLCA evidence, reported stored statistics and provenance in a compact table, and omitted off-disease, non-person, dramatic, and speculative language.

items.detail: prepare_memory_diff idempotently captures exact live host turns, derives source evidence and deterministic prior/posterior context, and creates no knowledge row before approval
items.summary: Seven focused tests passed: exact live turns are captured without sync, retries reuse one content-addressed source snapshot, preparation creates no knowledge row, approval persists the decision, and a fresh store retrieves it. The full 124-test suite, validator, lock check, and UI build also passed.
